what_it_does: This file is a descriptive terrain-entry ledger record for the voicemode Whisper speech-recognition engine, documenting its location, size, systemd service unit, launch script, config-driven runtime parameters, relationships to company services, and coverage/status metadata.
observations: The entry registers an external engine named voicemode at /home/tim/.voicemode, sized 2.9G, with status unconfirmed and no git remote.
It describes voicemode as the older standalone whisper.cpp ear, distinct from the GPU NeMo/transformers ears in ~/.voice-venvs.
The systemd unit voicemode-whisper.service v1.2.0 ExecStarts /home/tim/.voicemode/services/whisper/bin/start-whisper-server.sh.
Listen port and whisper model are configured in voicemode.env, not hardcoded in the unit file.
Coverage shows only one file read (voicemode-whisper.service) out of one total, with the concrete env file not inspected.
It is WantedBy default.target alongside Company services and linked to [[company]] and [[company-systemd]].
Tags include voice; secrets is false; move_intent is none; last_active is 2025-12-23.
standouts: Config-driven port and model live in the unread voicemode.env, so the entry deliberately records them as external rather than asserting values.
Direct single-hop launch chain: systemd unit → in-tree start-whisper-server.sh.
Explicitly framed as the legacy/baseline Whisper path predating the June voice-venv ears build.
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry frontmatter schema with register: descriptive, aliases, tags, relations, coverage, and status fields.
Uses wikilink relations such as [[company]] and [[company-systemd]] to connect to broader system entries.
Documents the service launch chain and separates runtime configuration from the unit file.
Includes a caveat when a referenced file (voicemode.env) was not read.
concerns: Status is unconfirmed and coverage is limited to a single service file.
Operational details (listen port, model name) are not verified because voicemode.env was not read.
No git remote and no breakdown of the 2.9G tree contents.
secrets: false is asserted without evidence in the file.
notes: A legacy voicemode Whisper STT terrain entry that tracks its systemd launch path and notes the port/model are env-driven rather than captured here.
questions: What are the exact port and whisper model values in voicemode.env?
Which clients or services consume this STT endpoint?
How does voicemode differ in capability or accuracy from parakeet, canary, and granite?
Is the service still actively used, given its older status and last_active date?
What does start-whisper-server.sh do and how does it handle failures?
purpose_vs_actual: The file states it is a descriptive terrain entry for the voicemode engine and does exactly that: it records identity, location, service wiring, config boundaries, and system relationships.
intention_signals: To preserve a precise, queryable record of the standalone voicemode Whisper STT service, distinguishing it from newer voice ears and flagging where live runtime details are stored outside the ledger.
novelty: It explicitly positions voicemode as the older baseline Whisper ear separate from the newer GPU NeMo/transformers voice venvs, and makes the unread voicemode.env a first-class caveat rather than guessing port/model.
contribution: It contributes the concrete identity, path, size, systemd unit, launch script, and config-source of the voicemode engine, plus its declared relation to company/company-systemd and the caveat that port/model are env-driven, which a fused view would retain as a distinct substrate record.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptive terrain entry for the voicemode whisper.cpp STT engine at /home/tim/.voicemode, documenting its systemd service, env-driven port and model, legacy status relative to newer voice ears, and relation to company services.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog the legacy voicemode Whisper speech-recognition service and its configuration boundaries within the company orienteering ledger.
